Title: Enroll a patient into a care program with a valid enrollment date
Preconditions: Logged in as clinician with Manage Program Enrollments privilege; patient exists with no active enrollment in the target program
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Open the Programs widget | 3. Click Enroll in a program | 4. Select an active program | 5. Set Date enrolled to today | 6. Save
Expected_Result: Patient is enrolled, the program appears as Active in the Programs list with correct enrollment date and the initial workflow state

Title: Reject enrollment when Date enrolled is in the future
Preconditions: Logged in as clinician; patient exists; target program is active
Test_Steps: 1. Open Programs widget | 2. Click Enroll | 3. Select a program | 4. Set Date enrolled to a future date | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a validation error stating enrollment date cannot be in the future; no enrollment is created

Title: Reject enrollment with Date enrolled before patient birthdate
Preconditions: Logged in as clinician; patient with known birthdate exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Programs widget | 2. Click Enroll | 3. Set Date enrolled earlier than the patient birthdate | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Validation error prevents save; enrollment date cannot precede patient birthdate

Title: Boundary: enroll with Date enrolled equal to today
Preconditions: Logged in as clinician; patient exists; target program active
Test_Steps: 1. Open Programs widget | 2. Enroll into a program | 3. Set Date enrolled exactly to today | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Enrollment is accepted at the boundary value of today and shows as Active

Title: Boundary: enroll with Date enrolled equal to patient birthdate
Preconditions: Logged in as clinician; patient with known birthdate exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Programs widget | 2. Enroll into a program | 3. Set Date enrolled equal to the birthdate | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Enrollment is accepted; enrollment on the birthdate is the inclusive lower boundary

Title: Prevent duplicate active enrollment in the same program
Preconditions: Patient already has an active (no completion date) enrollment in Program A
Test_Steps: 1. Open Programs widget | 2. Attempt to enroll the patient again into Program A | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System prevents a second concurrent active enrollment in the same program and shows an appropriate message

Title: Enroll patient concurrently into two different programs
Preconditions: Patient exists; two distinct active programs are configured
Test_Steps: 1. Enroll patient into Program A | 2. Enroll patient into Program B | 3. View Programs widget
Test_Data: Program A=HIV Care; Program B=TB Program
Expected_Result: Both enrollments are Active and listed independently with their own states and dates

Title: Re-enroll into a program after a prior completed enrollment
Preconditions: Patient has one completed (with completion date and outcome) enrollment in Program A
Test_Steps: 1. Open Programs widget | 2. Enroll patient into Program A again with date enrolled after the prior completion date | 3. Save
Expected_Result: A new active enrollment is created; history shows both the completed and the new active enrollment

Title: Reject overlapping enrollment dates within the same program
Preconditions: Patient has a completed enrollment in Program A spanning a date range
Test_Steps: 1. Enroll into Program A with date enrolled that falls inside the prior enrollment date range | 2. Save
Expected_Result: System rejects the overlapping enrollment with a validation error; no overlapping records persist

Title: Move enrollment to next workflow state
Preconditions: Patient enrolled in a program that has a workflow with multiple states
Test_Steps: 1. Open the enrollment | 2. Change the current workflow state to the next valid state | 3. Set state start date | 4. Save
Test_Data: Workflow=Treatment Status; FromState=Active; ToState=On ART; StateDate=today
Expected_Result: State transition is recorded, prior state is end-dated, and the new state shows as current

Title: State-transition matrix: valid and invalid transitions
Preconditions: Program with workflow states S1 (Screening), S2 (Active), S3 (On Treatment), S4 (Defaulted)
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt S1->S2 (valid) | 2. Attempt S2->S3 (valid) | 3. Attempt S3->S2 (verify policy) | 4. Attempt S1->S4 (skip) | 5. Record outcomes
Expected_Result: Allowed transitions persist; transitions disallowed by configuration are rejected; matrix behavior is consistent

Title: Reject state start date before enrollment date
Preconditions: Patient enrolled in a workflow-enabled program on a known date
Test_Steps: 1. Open enrollment | 2. Change workflow state | 3. Set state start date earlier than the enrollment date | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Validation error; a state cannot start before the program enrollment date

Title: Boundary: state start date equal to enrollment date
Preconditions: Patient enrolled in a workflow-enabled program
Test_Steps: 1. Open enrollment | 2. Set the first workflow state start date equal to the enrollment date | 3. Save
Expected_Result: State is accepted at the inclusive boundary equal to the enrollment date

Title: Prevent two concurrent active states in one workflow
Preconditions: Patient enrolled with an active workflow state
Test_Steps: 1. Add a second workflow state without end-dating the current one through a direct/API path | 2. Inspect states
Expected_Result: Only one state per workflow can be active at a time; the prior state is auto end-dated or the operation is rejected

Title: Complete an enrollment with a completion date and outcome
Preconditions: Patient has an active enrollment in a program that defines program outcomes
Test_Steps: 1. Open the active enrollment | 2. Set Date completed | 3. Select an Outcome concept | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Enrollment is marked completed, moves to history with the recorded outcome and completion date

Title: Reject completion date before enrollment date
Preconditions: Patient has an active enrollment with a known enrollment date
Test_Steps: 1. Open enrollment | 2. Set Date completed earlier than the enrollment date | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Validation error prevents save; completion date cannot precede enrollment date

Title: Reject completion date in the future
Preconditions: Patient has an active enrollment
Test_Steps: 1. Open enrollment | 2. Set Date completed to a future date | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Validation error; completion date cannot be in the future

Title: Boundary: completion date equal to enrollment date (same-day completion)
Preconditions: Patient enrolled today
Test_Steps: 1. Open enrollment | 2. Set Date completed equal to the enrollment date | 3. Select outcome | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Same-day completion is accepted at the inclusive boundary

Title: Outcome list is constrained to the configured outcomes concept set
Preconditions: Program configured with an outcomes concept set (e.g., Cured, Died, Transferred Out, Defaulted)
Test_Steps: 1. Open enrollment completion | 2. Open the Outcome dropdown | 3. Inspect available options
Expected_Result: Only the configured outcome concepts are selectable; values outside the set are not offered

Title: Auto end-date active workflow states on program completion
Preconditions: Patient enrolled with an active workflow state
Test_Steps: 1. Complete the program enrollment with a completion date | 2. Inspect prior active workflow states
Expected_Result: Active workflow states are end-dated on or before the completion date; no state remains open past completion

Title: Outcome of Died sets a clinically consistent terminal state
Preconditions: Patient enrolled in a program with a Died outcome and corresponding terminal state
Test_Steps: 1. Complete enrollment with outcome Died | 2. Inspect resulting workflow state and patient death attributes if configured
Expected_Result: Enrollment closes with terminal state; if configured, deceased linkage is consistent and no further state changes are allowed

Title: Transfer patient enrollment to another location
Preconditions: Patient has an active enrollment at Location A; Location B is enabled for the program
Test_Steps: 1. Open the active enrollment | 2. Change the enrollment location to Location B | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Enrollment location updates to B; history reflects the transfer with timestamp and user

Title: Transfer Out outcome closes local enrollment for inter-facility transfer
Preconditions: Patient has an active enrollment; Transferred Out is a configured outcome
Test_Steps: 1. Complete the enrollment with outcome Transferred Out | 2. Record destination facility note | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Enrollment is completed with Transferred Out; record supports continuity-of-care handoff

Title: Block enrollment when eligibility criteria are not met (age threshold)
Preconditions: Program restricted to patients meeting an age criterion via configured logic/cohort
Test_Steps: 1. Select a patient who does not meet the age criterion | 2. Attempt to enroll | 3. Observe result
Expected_Result: Enrollment is blocked or flagged per eligibility rule; non-eligible patient cannot be silently enrolled

Title: Decision table for eligibility combinations (age x diagnosis x gender)
Preconditions: Eligibility rule defined over age band, confirmed diagnosis, and gender
Test_Steps: 1. Evaluate eligible combination | 2. Evaluate age-fail combination | 3. Evaluate diagnosis-fail combination | 4. Evaluate gender-fail combination
Test_Data: Rule: age>=18 AND HIV+ AND any gender
Expected_Result: Each combination yields the rule-defined eligible/ineligible result consistently across the decision table

Title: Pairwise: program x location x role enrollment permissions
Preconditions: Multiple programs, locations and user roles configured with varying permissions
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt enrollment across pairwise combinations of program, location and role | 2. Record allowed/blocked
Test_Data: Programs x Locations x Roles pairwise set
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination enforces the configured permission; no unauthorized enrollment succeeds

Title: Edit enrollment date and verify state dates remain consistent
Preconditions: Patient enrolled with at least one workflow state
Test_Steps: 1. Open enrollment | 2. Change the enrollment date later than current first state date | 3. Save | 4. Observe validation
Test_Data: NewDateEnrolled later than first state date
Expected_Result: System prevents an enrollment date that postdates an existing state start date, or cascades consistently

Title: Void an enrollment with a reason and verify it is hidden
Preconditions: Patient has an enrollment; user has privilege to void enrollments
Test_Steps: 1. Open the enrollment | 2. Void it with a reason | 3. Confirm | 4. View Programs widget
Expected_Result: Enrollment is voided and removed from the active list while remaining recoverable in the database with the void reason

Title: Void cascades to associated workflow states
Preconditions: Enrollment has multiple workflow state records
Test_Steps: 1. Void the enrollment with reason | 2. Query patient_state and patient_program tables
Expected_Result: All child state rows are voided consistently with the parent enrollment; no orphaned active state remains

Title: Enrollment changes are captured with user, timestamp and old/new values
Preconditions: Audit/history logging enabled for program enrollments
Test_Steps: 1. Enroll a patient | 2. Change state | 3. Complete enrollment | 4. Inspect audit/history records
Expected_Result: Each change records actor, datetime, action and changed values supporting traceability

Title: Unauthorized role cannot enroll or modify enrollments
Preconditions: User with a role lacking Manage Program Enrollments privilege is logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient dashboard | 2. Attempt to access Enroll action | 3. Attempt direct enrollment via UI
Expected_Result: Enroll/modify actions are hidden or denied; no enrollment is created; access denial is logged

Title: Enrollment endpoint enforces authorization (no privilege escalation)
Preconditions: Authenticated session token for a low-privilege user
Test_Steps: 1. Send a direct enrollment request to the program endpoint as the low-privilege user | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Request is rejected with an authorization error; no enrollment persists; attempt is auditable

Title: Create a program enrollment via REST and verify fields
Preconditions: REST API enabled; valid token with program privilege; patient and program UUIDs known
Test_Steps: 1. POST to programenrollment with patient, program, dateEnrolled | 2. GET the created resource | 3. Validate fields
Expected_Result: 201/200 response; resource returns correct patient, program, dateEnrolled and active status

Title: Reject REST enrollment with invalid program UUID
Preconditions: REST API enabled; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. POST programenrollment with a non-existent program UUID | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Request fails with a 4xx error and a meaningful message; no enrollment is created

Title: Update workflow state via REST and verify transition
Preconditions: Existing enrollment with a workflow; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. POST a state change to the enrollment statuses endpoint | 2. GET enrollment | 3. Verify current state
Expected_Result: State updates successfully; prior state end-dated; response reflects the new current state

Title: Pagination and filtering of enrollments by program
Preconditions: Multiple enrollments exist across programs
Test_Steps: 1. GET enrollments filtered by program | 2. Apply limit and startIndex | 3. Verify counts
Expected_Result: Filtered, paginated results return only matching enrollments with correct total/links

Title: Map enrollment to FHIR EpisodeOfCare with active status
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint available via adapter; patient enrolled in a program
Test_Steps: 1. GET EpisodeOfCare for the patient | 2. Validate status, patient reference, period.start
Expected_Result: EpisodeOfCare returns status active, correct patient reference and period.start equal to enrollment date

Title: Completed enrollment maps to EpisodeOfCare status finished with period.end
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint available; patient has a completed enrollment
Test_Steps: 1. GET EpisodeOfCare | 2. Validate status and period.end
Expected_Result: EpisodeOfCare status is finished with period.end equal to the completion date

Title: CarePlan/CareTeam linkage reflects program workflow
Preconditions: FHIR adapter exposes CarePlan for program-based care
Test_Steps: 1. GET CarePlan associated with the EpisodeOfCare | 2. Validate status and category
Expected_Result: CarePlan references the EpisodeOfCare and reflects the active program with a valid category coding

Title: Validate code system URIs for program/outcome codings
Preconditions: FHIR resources expose program and outcome as codings
Test_Steps: 1. GET EpisodeOfCare/CarePlan | 2. Inspect coding.system and coding.code for program type and outcome
Expected_Result: Codings use valid canonical system URIs and codes; no placeholder or empty systems

Title: Outbound message conveys program enrollment change
Preconditions: HL7 interface engine configured to receive program updates
Test_Steps: 1. Enroll a patient | 2. Capture the outbound HL7 message | 3. Validate segments
Expected_Result: Message contains correct patient identifiers and program/encounter data per the interface spec

Title: Inbound enrollment update is processed idempotently
Preconditions: HL7 inbound feed mapped to program enrollment updates
Test_Steps: 1. Send an enrollment update message | 2. Resend the identical message | 3. Inspect enrollment records
Expected_Result: Duplicate inbound message does not create a second enrollment; processing is idempotent

Title: Referential integrity between patient_program and program tables
Preconditions: Database access to schema; existing enrollments
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect patient_program rows | 2. Verify program_id foreign keys resolve | 3. Attempt to remove a referenced program
Expected_Result: Foreign keys are enforced; a program referenced by enrollments cannot be hard-deleted leaving orphans

Title: Date fields persist with correct timezone handling
Preconditions: Server and client in different timezones; enrollment created
Test_Steps: 1. Enroll a patient at a known instant | 2. Query stored date_enrolled | 3. Compare to displayed value
Test_Data: TZ offset between client and server
Expected_Result: Stored and displayed dates are consistent and unambiguous; no off-by-one date shift

Title: Programs widget and enrollment form are keyboard navigable
Preconditions: Logged in as clinician on the patient dashboard
Test_Steps: 1. Tab to the Programs widget | 2. Open the Enroll form using keyboard only | 3. Complete and submit without a mouse
Expected_Result: All controls are reachable and operable by keyboard with a visible focus order; form submits successfully

Title: Enrollment form fields have accessible labels and error announcements
Preconditions: Screen reader available; enrollment form open
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate fields with a screen reader | 2. Trigger a validation error | 3. Verify announcement
Expected_Result: Each field exposes a programmatic label; validation errors are announced and associated with the field

Title: Program enrollment data is masked for unauthorized viewers
Preconditions: User role without rights to view sensitive program (e.g., HIV) data
Test_Steps: 1. Open the dashboard of an enrolled patient | 2. View Programs widget | 3. Inspect visibility of sensitive program
Expected_Result: Sensitive program enrollment is hidden or masked for the unauthorized role and access is logged

Title: Explore rapid enroll-complete-re-enroll cycles for state leakage
Preconditions: Clinician access; a workflow-enabled program
Test_Steps: 1. Enroll | 2. Transition states | 3. Complete | 4. Re-enroll quickly | 5. Observe widget and history for anomalies
Expected_Result: No stale active states, duplicate active enrollments or inconsistent history appear after rapid cycles

Title: Explore concurrent edits to the same enrollment by two users
Preconditions: Two clinicians can access the same patient enrollment simultaneously
Test_Steps: 1. User A opens enrollment | 2. User B opens the same enrollment | 3. Both edit and save | 4. Observe outcome
Expected_Result: Last-write or conflict handling is deterministic; no corruption or silent data loss; conflict surfaced if applicable

Title: Enroll using a program that is retired is not offered
Preconditions: A program has been retired in administration
Test_Steps: 1. Open Enroll form | 2. Open the program dropdown | 3. Search for the retired program
Expected_Result: Retired program is not selectable for new enrollments

Title: Workflow with no states defined enrolls without state errors
Preconditions: A program is configured with a workflow that has no concrete states or no workflow at all
Test_Steps: 1. Enroll patient into the program | 2. Open the enrollment
Expected_Result: Enrollment succeeds with no state widget errors; state section is empty or hidden gracefully

Title: Complete enrollment without selecting an outcome when outcome is required
Preconditions: Program is configured to require an outcome on completion
Test_Steps: 1. Open active enrollment | 2. Set completion date | 3. Leave outcome blank | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Validation requires an outcome before completion can be saved

Title: Complete enrollment with optional outcome left blank
Preconditions: Program does not require an outcome on completion
Test_Steps: 1. Open active enrollment | 2. Set completion date only | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Completion is accepted without an outcome when the program does not require one

Title: Enroll deceased patient is blocked or warned
Preconditions: Patient is marked deceased with a death date
Test_Steps: 1. Open the deceased patient | 2. Attempt to enroll into an active care program | 3. Observe behavior
Expected_Result: Enrollment of a deceased patient is blocked or strongly warned per policy; safety guard is enforced

Title: Enroll date after patient death date is rejected
Preconditions: Patient has a recorded death date
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt enrollment with date enrolled after the death date | 2. Save
Expected_Result: Validation rejects an enrollment date after the death date

Title: Pairwise: program type x enrollment status x location filter in list view
Preconditions: Patient has several enrollments across programs, statuses and locations
Test_Steps: 1. Apply pairwise filter combinations in the enrollment list/history | 2. Verify results
Expected_Result: Each pairwise filter combination returns the correct subset of enrollments

Title: Change outcome on an already completed enrollment
Preconditions: Patient has a completed enrollment with an outcome; user has edit privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open completed enrollment | 2. Change the outcome to another valid value | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Outcome updates; change is auditable; history reflects prior and new outcome

Title: Reopen a completed enrollment by clearing completion date
Preconditions: Patient has a completed enrollment; reopening is permitted by policy
Test_Steps: 1. Open completed enrollment | 2. Clear the completion date and outcome | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Enrollment returns to active status if policy permits; otherwise the action is blocked with a clear message

Title: State change requires Date in state within enrollment bounds
Preconditions: Patient enrolled with a completion date set on a completed program (edge)
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to add a state with a start date after the completion date | 2. Save
Expected_Result: State start date after completion is rejected; states must fall within enrollment bounds

Title: Enroll with a very long free-text note (field length boundary)
Preconditions: Enroll form includes an optional note field with a defined max length
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a note at maximum length | 2. Enter a note one character over the limit | 3. Save each
Expected_Result: Max-length note saves; over-limit note is truncated or rejected per the field constraint

Title: Reject special-character/script injection in enrollment note
Preconditions: Enroll form has a note field; XSS protections expected
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a script payload into the note | 2. Save | 3. Reopen the enrollment and view the note
Expected_Result: Payload is stored and rendered as inert text; no script executes; output is properly encoded

Title: Decision table: enrollment allowed by program-location mapping
Preconditions: Programs are mapped to permitted locations
Test_Steps: 1. Enroll at a permitted location | 2. Enroll at a non-permitted location | 3. Compare
Test_Data: Program A: allowed Loc1; not Loc2
Expected_Result: Enrollment succeeds only at permitted locations per the program-location decision table

Title: Voiding an enrollment is recorded with actor and reason in audit trail
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; enrollment exists
Test_Steps: 1. Void an enrollment with a reason | 2. Inspect the audit trail entry
Expected_Result: Audit trail captures the void action, actor, timestamp and reason supporting compliance review

Title: Concurrent API enrollment requests do not create duplicates
Preconditions: Valid token; patient with no active enrollment in target program
Test_Steps: 1. Fire two simultaneous create-enrollment requests for the same patient/program | 2. Inspect resulting records
Expected_Result: At most one active enrollment is created; the duplicate is rejected or de-duplicated

Title: State-transition: Defaulted to Restarted treatment after return-to-care
Preconditions: Patient enrolled with current state Defaulted and a Restarted/On Treatment state configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open enrollment | 2. Transition from Defaulted to Restarted treatment | 3. Set state date | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Transition is recorded; Defaulted state end-dated; Restarted becomes current; history is intact

Title: Program history is ordered chronologically and labeled by status
Preconditions: Patient has multiple completed and one active enrollment in a program
Test_Steps: 1. Open Programs widget history | 2. Review ordering and status labels
Expected_Result: History lists enrollments in chronological order with accurate Active/Completed labels and dates

Title: Pairwise: age band x diagnosis status x program eligibility outcome
Preconditions: Eligibility engine evaluates age band and diagnosis status
Test_Steps: 1. Evaluate representative pairwise combinations of age band and diagnosis status | 2. Record eligibility decision
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination returns the configured eligibility decision deterministically

Title: EpisodeOfCare history search returns active and finished episodes
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint exposes EpisodeOfCare for patients with multiple enrollments
Test_Steps: 1. Search EpisodeOfCare by patient | 2. Validate that active and finished episodes are returned with correct periods
Expected_Result: Search returns all episodes with correct status and period boundaries matching enrollment records

Title: Outcome concept change in admin does not corrupt historical outcomes
Preconditions: An outcome concept previously used is later renamed/retired in admin
Test_Steps: 1. Record an enrollment with the outcome | 2. Retire/rename the outcome concept | 3. Re-open the historical enrollment
Expected_Result: Historical enrollment retains its original recorded outcome reference; no corruption or blank outcome

Title: Direct object reference: user cannot view another patient enrollment by ID guessing
Preconditions: Two patients exist; user lacks rights to patient B
Test_Steps: 1. As an authorized user for patient A, request patient B enrollment by manipulating identifiers | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Access to patient B enrollment is denied; no PHI leaks; attempt is logged
